mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and improved earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market, which typically precedes a slowdown in consumer demand and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month period.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is low and housing starts (HOUST) are increasing, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy supporting a booming housing market, which is a significant driver of broader economic activity and positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative or flat when FEDFUNDS is below 2.5% and HOUST is above 1200.0 in more than 40% of triggered periods over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial production growth (INDPRO), corporate earnings tend to expand due to increased output and efficiency, leading to higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns, especially in an expansionary economic regime.
failure_condition: In periods officially classified as economic expansion, INDPRO month-over-month growth shows no significant positive correlation with SPX 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) often prompts central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and reduces corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns as investors anticipate higher rates.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are consistently positive or flat in more than 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports robust consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month growth and SPX 20-day returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong and unemployment (UNRATE) is low, it signifies a robust economy with high demand and full employment, creating an optimal environment for corporate profitability and sustained positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative or flat in more than 35% of periods where GDPC1 year-over-year growth exceeds 2% and UNRATE is below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) increase the cost of capital for businesses and reduce the present value of future earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns as investors re-price equities.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are consistently positive or flat during periods where FEDFUNDS has increased by more than 0.25% over a three-month period.
